context: Tests whether the agent presents skill recommendations in the correct structured format — skill name and description, install count and source, install command, skills.sh link — and uses the correct install command syntax including the -g and -y flags.

checklist.name: Skill name present
checklist.description: At least one recommended skill is identified by name

checklist.name: Skill description present
checklist.description: At least one recommended skill has a description of what it does

checklist.name: Install count stated
checklist.description: The install count (or an approximate number such as '100K+') is stated for at least one recommended skill

checklist.name: Publisher/source named
checklist.description: The source or publisher (e.g. 'vercel-labs', 'anthropics') is explicitly named for at least one recommended skill

checklist.name: Install command present
checklist.description: At least one ready-to-run install command starting with `npx skills add` is included

checklist.name: Install command -g flag
checklist.description: The install command includes the `-g` flag

checklist.name: Install command -y flag
checklist.description: The install command includes the `-y` flag

checklist.name: skills.sh link included
checklist.description: At least one URL pointing to skills.sh is included for learning more about the recommended skill

checklist.name: CLI find command used
checklist.description: Evidence (shown command or description) that `npx skills find` was used during the research process, not only a bare recommendation

checklist.name: Alternative terms tried
checklist.description: More than one search query is shown OR the document mentions trying an alternative keyword (e.g. 'e2e', 'playwright', 'jest') if the first term was broad
